title: FULL-DUPLEX FAIL: caller barges in mid-sentence, agent keeps talking through it (sustained talk-over)

transcript.agent: Your appointment window is Thursday between nine and eleven and the technician will bring the replacement part so the whole visit should take under an hour and you will get a text when...
transcript.caller: hold on, Thursday does not work for me, I need an afternoon slot, what do you have after two

why_it_matters: DELIBERATE BAD-AGENT RENDER, the bad twin of fdx-01. The caller takes the floor at 2.00 s and the agent keeps transmitting through more than two seconds of simultaneous speech before finally dropping its turn, so the floor does transfer eventually but far outside both bounds: the event FAILS on the talk-over bound (and on the yield-time bound). This is the failure mode specific to full-duplex operation: the agent can hear while it speaks, and the caller's channel shows a sustained floor-take, yet the agent's channel never stops. It is distinct from fd-01 in funnel-demo, where the agent never yields inside the search window at all; here the regression is the measured seconds of talk-over, the number a transcript-level eval cannot see.
